a: NOTA (None of the Above) is a ballot option on EVMs in India. If you select NOTA, you indicate none of the candidates are acceptable; it does not invalidate the election or trigger a re-poll. It is a symbolic protest vote.

q: How do I register as a new voter?
a: Use Form 6 on the Voter Helpline App or https://voters.eci.gov.in/ with age and address proof, or submit offline to your BLO/ERO.
Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) record your vote electronically. VVPAT prints a slip visible for 7 seconds for verification. ECI trains officers on secure handling and counting procedures.
Indian citizens aged 18+ on the qualifying date, ordinarily resident in the constituency, and not disqualified under law may register and vote.

Overseas Indians can register via Form 6A if eligible; they vote in person at their designated polling station in India (not postal for Lok Sabha general elections in normal rules—verify current ECI guidance).

q: What ID can I use at the polling booth?
a: EPIC is common but ECI allows 12 alternative photo IDs including Aadhaar, passport, driving licence, and others listed on the ECI website.

q: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
a: MCC is ECI guidelines for fair campaigning: restrictions on new announcements, misuse of official machinery, and corrupt practices during elections.
Votes are counted under ECI supervision with strongrooms, VVPAT slips sampled per ECI protocol, and results declared officially.

q: What is the voter helpline number?
a: National voter helpline: 1950. You can also use the Voter Helpline mobile app for registration status and booth information.
Certain service voters, special categories, and provisions defined by ECI/law may use postal ballot; check current ECI notifications for your category.
EPIC (Electors Photo Identity Card) is your voter ID number on the card issued by ECI; it helps identify you on the electoral roll.
Large states may hold polling in multiple phases for security and logistics; dates are announced in the ECI election schedule.

q: How do I object to a wrong entry in the roll?
a: Use Form 7 as prescribed by ECI and follow your CEO/ERO office process with supporting evidence.

q: How do I correct my voter details?
a: Use Form 8 for corrections or shifting within rules; online via voters portal or offline through BLO/ERO.
Local administration may impose orders restricting gatherings; always follow lawful orders and verify with local authorities—this is general civic information, not legal advice.
ECI may establish all-women managed booths in some areas for accessibility and comfort—availability varies by election and state.

q: What facilities exist for voters with disabilities?
a: ECI promotes ramps, wheelchairs, volunteers, and accessible materials; report gaps to the presiding officer or helpline 1950.
Higher turnout strengthens representative legitimacy; every eligible vote shapes outcomes within the constitutional framework.
The Election Commission of India conducts Lok Sabha and Assembly elections; State Election Commissions conduct local body polls per constitutional allocation.

q: Can I verify VVPAT with my vote?
a: VVPAT displays a paper slip for verification; ECI defines verification protocols during counting and audits—follow on-booth instructions from officials.
